g.text: Syscall Argument Register Mapping (x86_64 Linux ABI)
Syscall ABI (x86_64 Linux) — via syscall instruction
C Function ABI (System V AMD64) — via call instruction
Why r10 Instead of rcx for Arg 4?
Arg 4 | rcx | DIFFERENT — syscall uses r10
Step 1 | CPU saves RIP (return addr) → rcx
Step 3 | CPU jumps to MSR_LSTAR (kernel entry)
Result | rcx is CLOBBERED — holds return address, not arg4
Fix | Kernel ABI uses r10 for arg4 instead of rcx
Risk | Reading rcx for arg4 returns RIP — silent wrong data
Entry stop — syscall nr | regs.orig_rax | NOT regs.rax
Entry stop — arg1 | regs.rdi | pathname ptr
Entry stop — arg2 | regs.rsi | flags int
Entry stop — arg3 | regs.rdx | mode uint
Entry stop — arg4 | regs.r10 | NOT regs.rcx
Exit stop — retval | regs.rax | may be -errno
Exit stop — syscall nr | regs.orig_rax | kernel preserved
